description: OpenLan Gateway (OLG) uCentral schema for L3 routers

uuid.description: The unique ID of the configuration. This is the unix timestamp of when the config was created.

hostname.description: Hostname to set on the device.

router-id.description: Global router ID for routing protocols (BGP, OSPF, etc.).

domain-name.description: Default domain name for the device.

vlan-tag.description: Shall the port have a vlan tag.

vlan.description: This section describes the vlan behaviour of a logical network interface.

id.description: This is the pvid of the vlan that shall be assigned to the interface. The individual physical network devices contained within the interface need to be told explicitly if egress traffic shall be tagged.

proto.decription: The L2 vlan tag that shall be added (1q,1ad)

interface.description: Interface to apply this policy to.

name.description: Rule name for reference when binding to interfaces.

priority.description: Rule priority. Lower values evaluated first.

port.description: Source port or range.
Destination port or range.

protocol.description: IP protocol to match. Use "all" for all protocols and "tcp_udp" for both TCP and UDP.

inbound-interface.description: Match traffic arriving on this interface.

action.description: Action for matching traffic. Use either table OR next-hop, not both.

next-hop.description: Override next-hop directly without specifying a table.

ebgp-multihop.description: The number of hops to reach the neighbor.

default-originate.description: Advertise default route to this neighbor.

router-id.description: OSPF router identifier. Must be unique within the OSPF domain.

default-route-options.description: Options for default route in OSPF.

always-advertise.description: Always advertise default route even if not in routing table.

metric-type.description: External metric type. E1 => External cost + Internal OSPF cost E2 => External cost only

area-type.description: Area type. Stub/NSSA areas reduce LSA flooding.

no-summary.description: For stub/NSSA, block inter-area routes (totally stubby).

networks.description: Networks to include in this area (CIDR notation).

cost.description: OSPF cost for this interface. Lower is preferred.

passive.description: Advertise network but do not form adjacencies.

network-type.description: OSPF network type. Affects DR election and hello behavior.

priority.description: Router priority for DR/BDR election. 0 prevents becoming DR.

redistribute.description: Import routes from other sources into OSPF.

area-type.description: Area type. Stub/NSSA areas reduce LSA flooding.

no-summary.description: For stub/NSSA, block inter-area routes (totally stubby).

networks.description: Networks to include in this area (CIDR notation).

cost.description: OSPF cost for this interface. Lower is preferred.

passive.description: Advertise network but do not form adjacencies.

network-type.description: OSPFv6 network type. Affects DR election and hello behavior.

priority.description: Router priority for DR/BDR election. 0 prevents becoming DR.

redistribute.description: Import routes from other sources into OSPF.

state-policy.description: Global connection state policy applied before zone rules.

items.description: Firewall address, port, and MAC groups for reuse in rules.

name.description: Group name for reference in IPv4 rules.
Group name for reference in IPv6 rules.

port-groups.description: Groups of ports. Shared between IPv4 and IPv6 rulesets.

name.description: Group name for reference in rules.

mac-groups.description: Groups of MAC addresses for bridge firewall rules.

name.description: Group name for reference in bridge rules.

domain-groups.description: Groups of domain names. Shared between IPv4 and IPv6 rulesets.

name.description: Group name for reference in rules.
Ruleset name for reference in zone policies.

rule-number.description: Rule sequence number. Lower numbers evaluated first.

name.description: Ruleset name for reference in zone policies.

rule-number.description: Rule sequence number. Lower numbers evaluated first.

name.description: Ruleset name for reference in zone policies.

rule-number.description: Rule sequence number. Lower numbers evaluated first.

local-zone.description: Designate as local zone (traffic to/from router itself).

rule-number.description: Rule number for ordering (lower numbers processed first).

port.description: Source port or port range.

group.description: Reference to a firewall address/network/port group.

port.description: Destination port or port range.

group.description: Reference to a firewall address/network/port group.

address.description: Translated source address. Use 'masquerade' for dynamic IP.

port.description: Translated source port or port range.

rule-number.description: Rule number for ordering (lower numbers processed first).

port.description: Source port or port range.

group.description: Reference to a firewall address/network/port group.

port.description: Original destination port or port range.

group.description: Reference to a firewall address/network/port group.

port.description: Translated destination port or port range.

rule-number.description: Rule number for ordering (lower numbers processed first).

port.description: Source port or port range.

group.description: Reference to a firewall address/network/port group.

port.description: Destination port or port range.

group.description: Reference to a firewall address/network/port group.

port.description: Translated source port or port range.

rule-number.description: Rule number for ordering (lower numbers processed first).

port.description: Source port or port range.

group.description: Reference to a firewall address/network/port group.

port.description: Original destination port or port range.

group.description: Reference to a firewall address/network/port group.

port.description: Translated destination port or port range.

rule-number.description: Rule number for ordering (lower numbers processed first).

ipv6-prefix.description: Well-known prefix or custom NAT64 prefix.

ipv4-pool.description: IPv4 address pool for translated traffic.

address.description: IPv4 address or address range for source NAT.

name.description: Name identifier for this IKE group.

key-exchange.description: IKE version to use for key exchange.

hash.description: Hash algorithm for IKE SA integrity.

dh-group.description: Diffie-Hellman group number for key exchange.

action.description: Action to take when a dead peer is detected.

interval.description: Interval in seconds between DPD keepalive messages.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds before declaring a peer dead.

close-action.description: Action to take when the remote peer closes the connection.

name.description: Name identifier for this ESP group.

hash.description: Hash algorithm for ESP SA integrity.

id.description: List of local and remote IDs authorized to use this PSK.

name.description: Name identifier for this peer configuration.

local-address.description: Local IP address for the IPsec connection.

ike-group.description: Name of the IKE group to use for this peer.

esp-group.description: Name of the ESP group to use for this peer.

mode.description: Operating mode of the OpenVPN server.

persistent-tunnel.description: Keep the tunnel interface open across restarts.

address.description: Local IP address for the OpenVPN server to bind to.

port.description: Port number for the OpenVPN server to listen on.

encryption.description: Encryption settings for the VPN connection.

data-ciphers.description: Cipher negotiation list for data channel encryption.

topology.description: Virtual network topology for client addressing.

domain.description: DNS domain to push to clients.

max-connections.description: Maximum number of concurrent client connections.

name-server.description: DNS server address to push to clients.

address.description: Fixed IP address assigned to this client.

interface.description: Network interface to use for the VPN connection.

mode.description: Operating mode of the OpenVPN client.

encryption.description: Encryption settings for the VPN connection.

data-ciphers.description: Cipher negotiation list for data channel encryption.

persistent-tunnel.description: Keep the tunnel interface open across restarts.

remote-host.description: IP address or hostname of the remote OpenVPN server.

remote-port.description: Port number of the remote OpenVPN server.

private-key.description: Private key for this WireGuard interface.

address.description: IP address assigned to the WireGuard interface.

port.description: UDP port for WireGuard to listen on.

peers.description: List of peers for this WireGuard interface.

allowed-ips.description: List of IP ranges allowed through this peer.

address.description: IP address of the peer endpoint.

port.description: UDP port of the peer endpoint.

persistent-keepalive.description: Interval (in seconds) for sending keepalive packets to maintain NAT mappings.

target.description: IP to ping for health check.

networks.description: List of DHCP networks to serve.

default-router.description: Default gateway address for DHCP clients.

dns-servers.description: DNS server addresses to provide to clients.

domain-name.description: Domain name to provide to DHCP clients.

ranges.description: IP address ranges for dynamic allocation.

static-mappings.description: Static IP assignments based on MAC address.

ip-address.description: IP address to assign to this client.

listen-interfaces.description: Interfaces to listen for DHCP requests.

forwarding-servers.description: Upstream DNS servers for forwarding queries.

address.description: IP address of the upstream DNS server.

port.description: Port number of the upstream DNS server.

allow-from.description: Networks allowed to query this DNS server (CIDR notation).

listen-addresses.description: IP addresses the DNS server listens.

address.description: Hostname or IP address of the NTP server.

no-select.description: Use for timing but do not synchronize to this server.

nts.description: Enable Network Time Security (NTS) for this server.

pool.description: Treat this address as an NTP pool.

ptp.description: Enable Precision Time Protocol (PTP) for this server.

interleave.description: Enable interleaved mode for improved accuracy.

listen-ipv4-address.description: IPv4 address to listen on as NTP server.

listen-ipv6-address.description: IPv6 address to listen on as NTP server.

management-addresses.description: Management IP addresses to advertise via LLDP.

interface.description: Interface to listen as PPPoE server.

client-ip-pool.description: IP address pool for assigning to PPPoE clients.

wins-servers.description: Windows Internet Name Service (WINS) servers propagated to client.

service-names.description: Service-Name to respond. If absent, any Service-Name is acceptable and client's Service-Name will be sent back.

mtu.description: Maximum Transmission Unit used by PPPoE server

web-proxy.description: Proxy server with cache and filtering.

default-port.description: Port used for all specified listen-address.

ip.description: IP address that the proxy server listens.

port.description: Port number that the proxy server listens.

transparent-proxy.description: Automatically forward traffic arriving on port 80 destined for Internet.

non-cache-domains.description: Allow access to domains without retrieving them from the Proxy cache.

directory.description: Root directory for TFTP file transfers.

listen-address.description: IP address on which the TFTP server listens.

allow-upload.description: Enable uploads, else TFTP server will act as a read-only server.

alt-subnets.description: Additional subnets allowed to send multicast traffic.

traffic-policy.description: Traffic policing configuration (shaping, rate limiting, priority-based queues).

shaper.description: Traffic shaper policies for bandwidth management.

bandwidth.description: Guarantee bandwidth (in percentage) of the class.

burst.description: Size of token bucket (in bytes), which will be available to be sent at ceiling speed.

ceiling.description: Maximum speed possible for this class. Default is bandwidth value.

priority.description: Priority for usage of available bandwidth once guarantees have been met. The lower the priority number, the higher the priority.

listen-addresses.description: IP addresses that SNMP agent listens.

group.description: The group this user belong to.

interval.description: Interval (in seconds) for sending mark messages to the syslog input.

preserve-fqdn.description: Always send the domain part of the hostname.

address.description: IP address or hostname of the syslog server.

port.description: Port number of the syslog server.

facility.description: Syslog facility to use for messages sent to this server.

severity.description: Minimum severity level of messages to send to this server.

engine-id.description: Engine ID to identify this router.

sampling-rate.description: Packet sampling rate (1 in N packets).

address.description: IP address to use as the sFlow agent identifier.

sampling-rate.description: Packet sampling rate (1 in N packets).

address.description: IP address of the sFlow collector.

port.description: Port number of the sFlow collector.
